Title: 4-Coumarate 3-hydroxylase in the lignin biosynthesis pathway is a cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Zea mays; Brachypodium distachyon
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Brachypodium distachyon; Zea mays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (GC/MS; UPLC-MS/MS); published transcriptomics/microarray co-expression analyses; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes direct 3-hydroxylation of 4-coumarate to caffeate and functions as a cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase; reduced expression/activity lowers lignin deposition in Brachypodium and stronger Arabidopsis alleles reduce lignin and alter phenylpropanoid flux
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light stress in Arabidopsis metabolomics/transcriptomics; normal growth conditions otherwise
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced C3H causes stunted bushy growth, delayed senescence, few or non-viable seed in Brachypodium; homozygous null allele appears lethal in Arabidopsis; increased anthocyanins and altered xylem traits in Arabidopsis alleles
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lignin supports plant vascular function and stress responses
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification from maize roots with MS identification; recombinant enzyme kinetics; T-DNA mutants/RNAi; immunoblotting; lignin histochemistry and thioacidolysis; metabolite profiling; isotope labeling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily detected in lignifying vascular tissue; Arabidopsis APX1/C3H coexpressed with lignin, cell wall, glutathione-ascorbate cycle and stress-response genes; low expression in first internode reported for Brachypodium datasets
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of vascular and fiber cells in stems
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S enhancer present in Brachypodium activation-tagged line; RNAi constructs used in Arabidopsis, promoter details not specified
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Brachypodium activation-tag line likely downregulated due to methylation-mediated silencing of the quadruple 35S enhancer
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional enzyme links phenylpropanoid metabolism with H2O2 detoxification; only non-membrane-bound hydroxylase reported in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis; can also hydroxylate tyrosine to L-DOPA in vitro; may participate in higher-mass protein complex with TAL activity in maize
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a target to re-engineer lignin biosynthesis in forage and bioenergy crops; manipulation requires caution because strong loss of function causes severe growth/reproductive defects
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAL1 (co-eluting activity association); lignin pathway enzymes and glutathione-ascorbate cycle genes coexpressed
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase family; heme peroxidase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H was identified as a cytosolic ascorbate peroxidase with direct 4-coumarate 3-hydroxylase activity. 2. Genetic reduction of C3H lowers lignin and perturbs phenylpropanoid metabolism, especially in Brachypodium and stronger Arabidopsis alleles. 3. C3H defines a soluble phenolic-acid route into monolignol biosynthesis linked to oxidative stress metabolism.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin (G and S lignin via caffeate/ferulate intermediates)

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Brachypodium distachyon; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Brachypodium distachyon; Arabidopsis thaliana; Zea mays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell walls of vascular and fiber cells, especially stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides vascular function and contributes to stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early pathway step; branch-directing step diverting flux from H toward G/S lignin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Directly hydroxylates free 4-coumarate at the 3-position to produce caffeate, using ascorbate-peroxidase chemistry
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor for subsequent O-methylation to ferulate and downstream G/S monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/branch-directing step supported; direct rate-limiting designation not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis through a cytosolic route; reduced function causes lower lignin and accumulation of 4-coumarate
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light stress revealed reduced caffeate in Arabidopsis c3h1/apx1; ascorbate dependence and inhibition by reducing agents observed in assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical purification and MS identification; recombinant enzyme assays; mutant and RNAi analysis; metabolite profiling; lignin chemistry; isotope feeding context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected mainly in lignifying vascular tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with lignin/cell wall/stress genes; vascular tissue enriched
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Possible methylation-mediated silencing of quadruple 35S enhancer in Brachypodium activation-tagged line reduced expression
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TAL1-associated high-mass activity peak (putative complex association)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only non-membrane-bound hydroxylase in lignin pathway reported here; also oxidizes ascorbate at comparable catalytic efficiency; also hydroxylates tyrosine to L-DOPA in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tailor lignin amount/composition in bioenergy and forage crops, particularly grasses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H is a bifunctional cytosolic APX that converts 4-coumarate to caffeate. 2. C3H is genetically required for normal lignification, especially in grasses and severe Arabidopsis alleles. 3. The enzyme supports an alternative soluble entry route into monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong reduction causes growth defects, reproductive defects, and lethality in severe backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I heme peroxidase; cytosolic APX family

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support and stress-related vascular function
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phenolic acid O-methylation route downstream of caffeate
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early-to-midstream downstream of C3H in soluble acids pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for feruloyl-CoA and downstream monolignol synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: In grasses, supported as favored competing step for caffeate utilization; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High activity in Brachypodium supports efficient conversion of caffeate to ferulate and favors soluble acids pathway toward lignin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Compared in crude stem extracts under in vitro substrate concentrations of 10 and 50 µM caffeate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crude extract enzyme assays; product profiling in coupled assays; isotope feeding context
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose grasses favor methylation at caffeic acid level before CoA activation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for pathway engineering with C3H to redirect lignin flux in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT activity toward caffeate is much higher in Brachypodium than Arabidopsis. 2. This supports efficient caffeate-to-ferulate conversion in grasses. 3. COMT likely helps drive flux through the C3H-based soluble lignin pathway in grasses.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Parallel early-to-midstream route to caffeate/caffeoyl derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Parallel source of caffeate for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in parallel with C3H-derived acids pathway; combined reduction with C3H causes strong growth/lignin defects and double homozygote lethality in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic crossing; RNAi in mutant background; lignin analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in stronger Arabidopsis c3h alleles
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RNAi constructs used; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Absent or poorly conserved in many grasses; also annotated as lysophospholipase 2 and linked to oxidative stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Combined manipulation with C3H could strongly alter lignin, but severe fitness costs are likely
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSE functions in a parallel lignin route to the C3H acids pathway. 2. Simultaneous impairment of CSE and C3H in Arabidopsis is strongly deleterious and likely lethal as a double homozygote. 3. This demonstrates pathway redundancy and essentiality for lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double deficiency causes aborted seeds, growth defects, and reduced lignin
